name: The Credit Report Law Group
description_short: Law firm specializing in Fair Credit Reporting Act violations, credit report errors, and identity theft disputes. Operates on contingency with attorney's fees paid by defendants.
description_long:
The Credit Report Law Group is a New York-based law firm founded on the principle of consumer advocacy under federal credit reporting statutes. The firm positions itself as distinct from traditional "credit repair" companies by offering legal representation rather than dispute services, with a stated philosophy of combining professionalism and consumer protection against corporate abuse. Led by attorneys Adam G. Singer and Howard Goralnick, the firm handles cases involving inaccurate credit reports, identity theft, mixed files, employment/criminal background check errors, tenant screening errors, and data breaches—all rooted in violations of the Fair Credit Reporting Act and Fair Credit Billing Act.

The firm's service model centers on litigation and legal advocacy rather than credit report dispute filing. They represent consumers in federal court (including Manhattan Federal Court) and pursue settlements against credit bureaus and financial institutions. A key distinction is their contingency-based fee structure: because federal and state consumer statutes allow the "wrongdoer to pay attorney's fees," the firm can represent clients without upfront costs. This model enables consumers to access legal representation for credit-related violations that might otherwise be cost-prohibitive.

What distinguishes The Credit Report Law Group is their emphasis on litigation outcomes and damage compensation rather than dispute-and-monitor approaches. Client testimonials highlight hands-on attorney involvement, meticulous case preparation, and detailed client communication throughout litigation. The firm explicitly markets itself as "better than credit repair," suggesting they view their legal advocacy as more powerful than traditional dispute mechanisms. They also offer free credit report disclosures to help clients identify violations before engaging legal services.

The primary honest caveat is that this is a litigation-based legal service, not a credit repair or credit building service. Success depends on identifying actionable violations of federal law (FCRA, FCBA) rather than simply correcting reporting errors. This means clients must have legitimate legal claims; the firm is not appropriate for consumers seeking to remove accurate negative information or build credit from scratch. Geographic limitation to New York representation and federal court jurisdiction may also restrict accessibility for some consumers.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Contingency fee model means no upfront attorney costs—wrongdoers pay legal fees per federal statute
Litigation-focused approach pursues actual compensation and restitution, not just dispute correction
Meticulous case preparation and detailed client communication throughout process, per testimonials
Expertise in federal credit law (FCRA, FCBA) with documented success against major banks and corporations
Handles multiple related issues: credit errors, identity theft, mixed files, background check errors, data breaches
Free credit report disclosure review to identify potential violations before legal engagement
Track record of federal court settlements with confidential terms, indicating significant recoveries
cons: Litigation-based model requires actionable federal law violations; cannot remove accurate negative information or function as traditional credit repair
Geographic focus on New York and federal jurisdiction may limit representation for consumers in other states
Success outcomes depend on strength of legal claim, not guaranteed credit score improvement or correction speed
No indication of success rates or typical settlement ranges on website, making ROI expectations unclear for prospective clients
Not appropriate for consumers seeking quick credit building or monitoring; this is adversarial legal action requiring time and patience
best_for: Consumers with verifiable credit report errors caused by creditor/bureau negligence or FCRA violations who want legal compensation
Identity theft victims seeking restitution and damages beyond simple dispute resolution
Individuals wrongly denied employment or housing due to background check errors or mixed credit files
diagnosis: The Credit Report Law Group is best for consumers who have suffered measurable harm from credit reporting violations and seek legal compensation, not credit repair. The critical caveat is that this is litigation-based legal advocacy requiring a valid federal law violation claim—it is not a credit repair service and cannot remove accurate negative items or provide quick credit score fixes.
services: Credit report error litigation under Fair Credit Reporting Act
Identity theft legal representation and damage claims
Mixed file credit report disputes and correction
Employment and criminal background check error litigation
Tenant screening and housing background check error disputes
Fair Credit Billing Act violation claims
Free credit report disclosure review and analysis
Federal court litigation and settlement negotiation
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
